goal: Remove every always-on service from `surface` so the stack survives the laptop being off. Always-on load moves to the local Mac fleet (M4 + 2× M2) and Hetzner; Surface keeps only dev + an optional on-demand 30B power model.

legend.always_on: must run 24/7 for the system to function
legend.on_demand: fine to be up only when the workstation/user is active

workloads.why_move: Every agent + app routes through it — the single most critical always-on dependency. Keep on local M4 so provider API keys stay on the LAN (not in cloud).
workloads.notes: Lightweight Python proxy. Port stays dynamic via port_broker → services.json. API keys move to the M4's user env (setup-litellm-keys equivalent for macOS).

workloads.why_move: 30B CANNOT fit the Mac fleet (30B Q4 ≈ 16-18GB > M4 16GB + macOS). Always-on local floor becomes an 8-14B on the M4; the 30B stays on Surface as an OPTIONAL on-demand power tier.
workloads.notes: LiteLLM `local-fallback` repoints to the M4 llama-server. Add a separate `power-local` alias for Surface's 30B that is only in rotation when Surface is up.

workloads.why_move: Keep the GPU-class model as a bonus when the laptop is on; nothing depends on it. Falls out of the always-on path entirely.
workloads.notes: LiteLLM health-checks it; when Surface is off, router skips it and uses M4 floor / free clouds.

workloads.why_move: Reads usage.db (written by LiteLLM callback) — co-locate with the gateway on the M4 so the dashboard is up whenever the gateway is.
workloads.notes: stdlib Python, binds 127.0.0.1. Expose on LAN only if you want to view from the workstation (bind 0.0.0.0 + firewall, or SSH tunnel).

workloads.why_move: Telegram-driven pilot — if it must answer 24/7, runs on the M4; if only during work sessions, can stay on-demand. Decision below.
workloads.notes: Needs LiteLLM up (now on M4). Brain = google/gemini-3-flash-preview via gateway.

workloads.host_target: OPEN QUESTION — vault lives on the laptop
workloads.why_move: Can't be laptop-independent while the source vault is on the laptop. Options: (a) keep ingest on-demand (only when laptop on); (b) relocate/mirror the canonical vault to SanDisk-on-a-Mac or a synced location an M2 watches; (c) push-on-save from the laptop to a Mac ingest worker.
workloads.notes: Resolve before claiming full laptop-independence for the KG ingest path.

workloads.host_target: neo4j → m4-16gb OR hetzner (if upgraded); app → on-demand / Hetzner
workloads.why_move: If the KG should be queryable 24/7, Neo4j must leave the laptop. M4 has RAM headroom now hosting gateway+8B; Hetzner CX22 does not (needs CX32+).
workloads.notes: Neo4j+APOC ~2GB. Sprint 2 jina-v4 re-embed (768→1024) is a natural moment to relocate.

workloads.why_move: Lightweight, perfect for the always-on M2 edge node; offloads transcription from the laptop.
workloads.notes: Echo app's transcribe path can call the M2 node.

workloads.why_move: Hetzner already runs prod n8n + Langfuse v2. The local Surface copies are redundant — decommission them; point LiteLLM tracing at Hetzner Langfuse (logs.synergify.com).
workloads.notes: Memory says local Langfuse stack was already meant to be decommissioned. Finish that.

workloads.host_target: hetzner (if upgraded) OR m4 OR drop
workloads.why_move: Decide per actual usage. If web-archive/bookmarks must be 24/7, move to a cloud/M4 node; otherwise keep on-demand or drop.
workloads.notes: Both are optional-ish; don't move what you don't use.

workloads.why_move: Local floor already moved to llama-server (GGUF). Ollama is legacy convenience — retire to free RAM.
workloads.notes: Per memory, llama-server is the floor now.

workloads.why_move: Already correctly placed in the cloud tier.
workloads.notes: Public ingress via CF tunnel ENERV-HUB.
